articles.summary: A study by researchers at World Peace University reveals that nearly 35% of dragonfly and damselfly species in the Western Ghats are facing extinction. The decline is attributed to pollution, habitat destruction, and climate change. These insects are vital indicators of freshwater health, and their loss signals a broader ecological crisis in the biodiversity hotspot.

articles.headline: Sun Pharma bids $13 billion for Organon, aiming for global drugmaking powerhouse
articles.summary: Dilip Shanghvi's Sun Pharma submitted a binding $13 billion offer for NYSE-listed Organon, competing with EQT and Gruenthal. The deal, financed by JP Morgan, MUFG, and Citi with $12 billion, would merge Organon with Sun, transforming it into a global branded and innovative drugmaker. Organon's stock surged 52% on buzz.

articles.headline: India's private sector activity accelerates in April, manufacturing leads upturn
articles.summary: India's private sector activity rose in April, with the Purchasing Managers Index (PMI) hitting 58.3. This acceleration, supported by stronger demand, particularly in manufacturing, follows a slowdown in March. Employment growth also strengthened, reaching a 10-month high.

articles.headline: FM Sitharaman discusses AI model threats, cybersecurity with bankers
articles.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman and IT Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw met bankers to discuss threats from AI models like Claude Mythos, emphasizing high vigilance. They called for real-time threat intelligence sharing among banks and CERT-In, urging proactive measures to secure IT systems and customer data.

articles.headline: Fuel sales surge on fears of price hike post-elections
articles.summary: Petrol and diesel sales surged over 13% year-on-year from April 1-21, driven by fears of a price hike after state elections conclude on April 29. Indian Oil Corp assured normal supplies despite panic buying in some areas. The petroleum ministry denied any proposal for a price hike.

articles.headline: Jubilant FoodWorks in talks to sell Dunkin' India rights to Inspire Brands
articles.summary: Jubilant FoodWorks (JFL) is negotiating to sell Dunkin's India franchise rights to US-based Inspire Brands. This comes ahead of the formal termination of JFL's franchise deal with Dunkin' in December 2026. Inspire Brands acquired US-based Dunkin' in 2020.

articles.headline: Infosys Q4 profit jumps 21% to Rs 8,051 crore, revenue up 13.4%
articles.summary: India's second-largest IT firm, Infosys, reported a 20.8% YoY jump in Q4 net profit to Rs 8,051 crore and a 13.4% rise in revenue to Rs 46,402 crore. Despite beating profit expectations, revenue missed estimates. FY27 revenue guidance of 1.5-3.5% signals uncertainty from West Asia conflict and AI-led compression.

articles.headline: India and Qatar discuss boosting trade and supply chain resilience
articles.summary: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal and Qatar's Minister of State for Foreign Trade Affairs Ahmed bin Mohammed Al Sayed held virtual talks to enhance bilateral trade and investment. Discussions also focused on strengthening supply chain resilience amidst disruptions caused by the West Asia crisis.

articles.headline: India caught in US-Iran crossfire over Trump's "hellholes" comment
articles.summary: India found itself in a US-Iran diplomatic spat after President Trump reposted a comment calling India and China "hellholes" due to birthright citizenship. Iran retaliated by calling them "cradles of civilisation." India chose not to react to the controversial post, while the US embassy made a conciliatory statement.

articles.headline: Rajnath Singh urges Germany to partner with India for defense systems
articles.summary: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh, visiting Germany, highlighted India's potential for co-development of weapon systems, offering risk diversification for Germany in global supply chains. He identified advanced radar, AI-enabled drones, and underwater transmitters as key cooperation areas under "ReArm Europe" and "Aatmanirbhar Bharat" initiatives.

articles.headline: Congress questions government's fuel management amidst falling production
articles.summary: Congress leader Mallikarjun Kharge criticized the Modi government's fuel management, noting crude oil production fell for the 11th year and gas production also declined. He alleged new LPG connections stopped, and farmers suffered from fertilizer shortages, accusing the government of denying realities despite public suffering.

articles.headline: India-Africa partnership sends stability message amidst global turbulence, says EAM
articles.summary: External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar stated that India's partnership with Africa conveys stability in a turbulent world, focusing on meaningful transformation beyond economic growth. Speaking at the launch of the India-Africa Forum Summit, he noted its significance amid geopolitical challenges and exploration of new frontiers in digital technology.

articles.headline: Gram panchayats boost own revenue for local development, reducing government reliance
articles.summary: Gram panchayats across India are increasingly raising their own revenue (OSR) for local needs. Maharashtra's Vasambe Panchayat surged tax collection by 307% in FY25, funding a computer lab. Karnataka's Belle GP achieved 100% digital tax collection, while Tripura's Bijoynagar GP boosted OSR by 77% for female digital literacy. This aligns with the 16th Finance Commission's grants tied to OSR.

articles.headline: Karnataka open to bike taxi policy despite Supreme Court appeal against HC ruling
articles.summary: Karnataka's Congress government is considering a policy for platform-based bike taxis, despite appealing a High Court ruling that allowed services to resume. This follows AICC leader Rahul Gandhi's letter to CM Siddaramaiah, expressing concern for thousands of riders. The state argues the HC ruling overlooked regulatory discretion on vehicle registration.

articles.headline: Karnataka launches 'HT Mitra' digital portal for large electricity consumers' grievances
articles.summary: Karnataka Energy Minister KJ George inaugurated Bescom’s 'HT Mitra', a digital grievance redressal portal for 18,000+ high tension (HT) electricity consumers. Developed in-house, it ensures faster, transparent complaint resolution with real-time tracking, aiming to improve service delivery and ease of doing business for industries and commercial establishments.

articles.headline: Indian Oil confirms no fuel shortage in Karnataka, despite panic buying
articles.summary: Siddhartha Agarwal, Indian Oil ED, stated there is no petrol or diesel shortage in Karnataka, with normal retail outlet operations. He affirmed a robust supply chain and adequate LPG supplies, noting easing panic buying from March's surge. Digital LPG bookings now account for 94.5%.

articles.headline: Baramati and Rahuri assembly bypolls record over 50% voter turnout
articles.summary: Bypolls for Baramati and Rahuri assembly seats in Maharashtra saw over 50% voter turnout. Sunetra Pawar's election in Baramati is largely seen as a formality. However, her son Jay Pawar's interest in contesting the 2029 elections has sparked speculation about a potential family contest.

articles.headline: Bhowanipur constituency gears up for Mamata vs Adhikari electoral battle
articles.summary: Business is slow in Kolkata's Jadubabur Bazar as Bhowanipur prepares for elections on April 29. The constituency, historically a Mamata Banerjee stronghold, will see her contest against Suvendu Adhikari, who famously defeated her in Nandigram in 2021. The area shows a mix of political posters and local life.

articles.headline: PM Modi praises high voter turnout and peaceful polls in West Bengal
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi commended the "bumper" voting in West Bengal's first phase, especially by youth and women, and lauded the ECI for ensuring largely peaceful polls. He asserted that the high turnout signals a strong mandate for change, anticipating BJP's victory in the state.

articles.headline: Mamata Banerjee leads vibrant roadshow in Bhabanipur constituency
articles.summary: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee led a colourful one-km roadshow in her Bhabanipur constituency in south Kolkata. Joined by TMC workers and Mayor Firhad Hakim, she greeted crowds near her Kalighat residence, interacting with children and the elderly.

articles.headline: ED argues officers can approach Supreme Court under Article 32 for law and order breach
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) contended in the Supreme Court that its officers can invoke Article 32 if law and order is breached, citing violation of Article 14 in West Bengal. Solicitor General Tushar Mehta argued illegal coal smuggling worth Rs 2,700 crore and sought protection for officers discharging statutory duties, clarifying it's not a complete constitutional breakdown.

articles.headline: Delhi HC orders takedown of Kejriwal's recusal hearing clips, issues contempt notice
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court ordered social media platforms to remove videos of court proceedings where Arvind Kejriwal and others sought Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma's recusal. The court issued a notice to Kejriwal and X on a contempt plea, affirming that unauthorized recording and circulation of court proceedings violate rules.

articles.headline: Railways to fast-track bullet train projects with bundled clearances
articles.summary: Indian Railways will implement future bullet train corridors on a mission mode, seeking bundled clearances across central, state, and local authorities. This aims to speed up land acquisition, right-of-way clearances, and improve manufacturing supply chains through design standardization and pre-cast civil works. Seven new corridors are planned.

articles.headline: Fertilizer bodies declare indefinite shutdown in Maharashtra over enforcement drive
articles.summary: The Maharashtra Fertilizers, Pesticides, and Seeds Association (MFPSA) called for an indefinite statewide shutdown, warning that an enforcement drive risks disrupting agri-input supplies. Ten national agri-input associations also announced a one-day shutdown on April 27 in solidarity.

articles.headline: Author questions EC's unilateral rule-setting, warns of eroding democratic legitimacy
articles.summary: Political scientist Pippa Norris's study on electoral integrity highlights that unilateral rule-setting by actors who gain electorally collapses democratic competition. The author argues India is failing this, citing flawed delimitation and the EC's discretionary voter list revisions. These actions risk eroding political legitimacy and voter participation.

articles.headline: Meditation extends subjective experience of time, fostering longevity perception
articles.summary: Chetan Savla explores how our perception of time varies with engagement. Stimulating activities make time fly, while boredom slows it. Meditation, by focusing on minute temporal units without thoughts or sense pleasures, allows a slower, lingering experience of time, creating a subjective sense of longevity.

articles.headline: Public outrage against political disruptions highlights demand for civic order
articles.summary: The article discusses a woman's furious confrontation with a minister over traffic caused by a rally in Mumbai, reflecting public frustration with political theatre disrupting daily life. It argues that constant encroachments and blockades, often normalized as "celebration of democracy," inflict collateral damage on civic order and demand accountability from public officials.

articles.headline: Consumers shift focus to lasting value over disposability in purchasing decisions
articles.summary: A growing consumer segment is moving away from disposable consumption towards lasting investments, driven by value defined beyond price. McKinsey-BoF's report notes demand for permanent items, with jewellery seeing a rise in higher-priced, fewer items. Consumers now seek transparency and longevity, viewing purchases as investments rather than fleeting trends.

articles.headline: India needs broader entrepreneurial focus beyond VC-backed tech startups
articles.summary: The article argues India's entrepreneurial focus is too narrow, centered disproportionately on VC-funded tech startups and AI. It advocates for nurturing world-class companies in core sectors like infrastructure, healthcare, and manufacturing that serve domestic markets, emphasizing patient, disciplined growth over rapid blitz-scaling.

articles.headline: JSW Group plans to monetize Akzo Nobel India's non-core land assets for up to Rs 4,500 crore
articles.summary: JSW Group, which acquired a majority stake in Akzo Nobel India (now JSW Dulux) for Rs 9,000 crore, plans to monetize its non-core land assets. Proceeds could range from Rs 4,000-4,500 crore, either through third-party sale or JSW Realty, to recover up to 50% of the acquisition cost.

articles.headline: Lavasa homebuyers appeal to NCLT, alleging unfair terms in resolution plan
articles.summary: Homebuyers in the Lavasa Corporation insolvency case filed a fresh appeal to the NCLT, alleging discriminatory treatment, arbitrary financial burden, and lack of transparency in the resolution plan approved by the Welspun Group-Ashdan Properties consortium. They seek a stay on the NCLT order and compensation with 12% interest.

articles.headline: India's aviation traffic projected to reach 500 million by 2030, 1 billion by 2047
articles.summary: India's aviation sector is set to handle 500 million passengers annually by 2030 and nearly 1 billion by 2047, driven by sustained demand and infrastructure growth. With over 160 operational airports and continued investments, aviation contributes $54 billion and millions of jobs. Navi Mumbai Airport aims for 90 million passengers by 2032.

articles.headline: Iranians now blame US-Israel for economic woes, not Tehran, says Djavad Salehi-Isfahani
articles.summary: Economics Professor Djavad Salehi-Isfahani explains Iran's 'resistance economy' and the shift in public blame for economic difficulties. Previously blaming the government, Iranians now attribute their struggles to the US-Israel conflict, seeing Tehran as the solution rather than the problem amidst economic pressures and war.

articles.headline: Tesla's AI ambitions to cost additional $25 billion in capital expenditure
articles.summary: Tesla anticipates over $25 billion in capital expenditures for 2026, significantly up from last year's outlay. This spending supports Elon Musk’s vision to transform Tesla into an AI and robotics company, funding factory expansions, Optimus humanoid robots, AI initiatives, and autonomous vehicle development.

articles.headline: US weekly jobless aid applications inch up to 214,000
articles.summary: The number of Americans filing for unemployment benefits rose by 6,000 to 214,000 for the week ending April 18. This figure is slightly higher than analysts' estimates but remains within the historically healthy range of recent years, according to the Labor Department.

articles.headline: US government eases access to marijuana for medical reasons
articles.summary: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche announced that the US government has reclassified marijuana as a drug with moderate to low potential for physical and psychological dependence. This move is aimed at expanding patients' access to treatments using cannabis for medical reasons.

articles.headline: Warner-Paramount $81 billion merger gets shareholder approval
articles.summary: Shareholders have approved the $81 billion Warner-Paramount mega merger, advancing a deal that could reshape Hollywood. While approved by shareholders, the acquisition still faces regulatory reviews. A separate measure on post-merger executive payments was rejected by Warner shareholders.

articles.headline: Tesla becomes Intel's first major customer for next-generation 14A chip technology
articles.summary: Tesla CEO Elon Musk announced the EV maker plans to use Intel's next-generation 14A manufacturing process to make chips for its Terafab AI chip complex in Austin. This contract marks Intel's first major customer for its advanced chipmaking technology, a significant breakthrough for the chipmaker.

articles.headline: Netflix plans $25 billion stock buyback amidst declining shares
articles.summary: Netflix's board approved an additional $25 billion stock buyback program, adding to $6.8 billion available from a previous authorization. This decision comes after a disappointing financial quarter and follows declining shares. The company repurchased 13.5 million shares for $1.3 billion in Q1.

articles.headline: India's FMCG market reshaped by changing consumption, emerging brands
articles.summary: India's FMCG market, valued at Rs 20.73 lakh crore, is undergoing a structural shift. Consumers are more aware, seeking healthier, ingredient-led, and functional products, moving away from mass messaging towards relatability. Startups are thriving in niche segments, guided by data and technology, leveraging e-commerce and quick commerce for faster iteration and brand affinity.

articles.headline: Prime residential prices in India rise, reducing space $1M can buy
articles.summary: Prime residential prices in Mumbai, Delhi, and Bengaluru continued to rise in 2025, decreasing the square footage $1 million can purchase. Mumbai now buys 1,033 sq ft (down from 1,066 in 2024), and Delhi 2,207 sq ft (from 2,239). India's growing share of global wealth drives demand for prime housing.

articles.headline: Flexible workspace operators secure large office space deals in Q4
articles.summary: Major flexible workspace operators, including Smartworks and WeWork, leased nearly 2 million sq ft of office space in Q4, driven by growing demand from large enterprises. This indicates a structural shift in occupier strategy, with flexible space penetration in India projected to exceed 12-15% of total office stock in coming years.

articles.headline: Karnataka partners with Ideabaaz Tech to mentor startups and enhance market linkages
articles.summary: Karnataka's IT/BT department, via KITS, signed an MoU with Ideabaaz Tech to provide mentorship, investor access, and market linkages for startups. This aims to strengthen the state's innovation hub status, supporting startups from early-stage validation to market entry and scale, improving access to networks, infrastructure, and funding.

articles.headline: WeWork India leases over 204,000 sq ft office space in Pune's Wakad
articles.summary: WeWork India has leased more than 204,000 sq ft of office space across five floors in Phoenix Millennium Tower 3 in Pune’s Wakad. The long-term agreement includes a monthly rent of Rs 73 per sq ft and a security deposit of Rs 4.47 crore, reflecting sustained leasing momentum in Pune's office market.

articles.headline: India's billionaire population projected to rise 51% to 313 by 2031
articles.summary: A Knight Frank report forecasts India's ultra-high net worth individuals (UHNWIs) to reach 25,217 and billionaires 313 by 2031, up from 19,877 and 207 currently. This acceleration in wealth creation is spurred by growth in technology, industrials, and capital markets, solidifying India's role in the global wealth landscape.

articles.headline: Indian equities fall nearly 1% as Brent oil prices cross $100
articles.summary: India's stock gauges declined almost 1% on Thursday, tracking Asian losses, as Brent oil prices surpassed $100, fueling cautious sentiment. NSE Nifty fell 0.8% to 24,173.05, and BSE Sensex declined 1.1% to 77,664. Analysts predict consolidation in the 23,700-24,500 range without strong triggers.

articles.headline: Pharma stocks rally, Dr Reddy's surges 9.4% on Semaglutide approval expectations
articles.summary: Pharma stocks defied market weakness, with the Nifty Pharma Index gaining 2.4%. Dr Reddy’s Laboratories soared 9.4% amid expectations of Canadian approval for its Semaglutide generic. Other firms in the Semaglutide value chain, OneSource and Shaily Engineering, also surged. Defensive positioning attracts investors amid uncertain market outlook.

articles.headline: Crude derivatives trading surges in India amid global oil price rally
articles.summary: Retail trading in India’s crude derivatives surged due to rising global oil prices from the US-Iran conflict. Average daily lots traded in crude oil futures on MCX jumped nearly four-fold in March. Trading conditions tightened with exchange margins climbing to almost 48%, reflecting heightened volatility and speculative interest.

articles.headline: Rupee breaches 94/$ mark for first time in April amidst RBI policy rollback
articles.summary: The rupee declined for the fourth consecutive session, closing at 94.11/$ on Thursday, after RBI rolled back foreign exchange trade curbs. Elevated Brent crude prices, surpassing $100 for the first time in over two weeks, also contributed to the currency's weakness.

articles.headline: Wall Street retreats from record highs after mixed earnings, rising oil
articles.summary: US stock markets fell Thursday, with the S&P 500 down 0.7%, following mixed corporate earnings and rising oil prices amidst West Asia conflict uncertainty. The Dow Jones and Nasdaq also declined, marking a stumble after a weeks-long rally had pushed indices to record highs.

articles.headline: West Asia conflict solidifies US dollar's dominance in global trade
articles.summary: The ongoing conflict in West Asia is strengthening the US dollar's role in global trade, with its share in international transactions hitting a record 51.1% in March, according to Swift data. Despite previous dollar weakness, its position as a reserve currency remains strong, driven by demand amidst rising energy prices.

articles.headline: RBI warns of West Asia crisis shocks, potential demand, financial turmoil
articles.summary: RBI's state of the economy report warns that a prolonged West Asia conflict could lead to higher energy costs, supply chain disruptions, and financial market spillovers, raising inflation risks. Retail inflation rose to 3.4% in March, driven by food and fuel. The report emphasizes continuous assessment for potential second-round effects.

articles.headline: HDFC alerts clients against using cards for overseas capital account transactions
articles.summary: HDFC Bank warned customers against using credit, debit, or forex cards for overseas capital account transactions like buying stocks or properties abroad, as it violates foreign exchange and banking rules. Such transactions mandate "proper banking channels" and non-compliance attracts RBI penalties.

articles.headline: IT sector sees steady margins, deal flow but project delays impact Q4 revenue
articles.summary: Top IT players like Infosys and HCLTech reported strong FY26 top-line growth but saw worse-than-expected sequential revenue declines in Q4 due to delayed project rollouts. Weak FY27 guidance, coupled with AI disruption risks and falling price-earnings multiples, suggest continued pressure and adjustments for the sector.

articles.headline: Liberalised Remittance Scheme outflows dip to $2.3 billion in February
articles.summary: Outward remittances under India's Liberalised Remittance Scheme (LRS) decreased slightly to $2.3 billion in February from $2.6 billion in January. Indians spent $1.3 billion on travel and $266 million on maintenance for relatives abroad, while remittances for studies abroad declined to $175 million.

articles.headline: Yuan projected to overtake yen in dollar currency options trading by 2028
articles.summary: The yuan is expected to become the second-most traded currency against the dollar in foreign-exchange options by 2028, surpassing the yen, according to LCH. This reflects the "real" internationalisation of the yuan, driven by China's economic growth and government measures supporting its globalisation.

articles.headline: RBI approves Vivek Tripathi's elevation as AU SFB Executive Director
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India approved Vivek Tripathi's elevation as Executive Director of AU Small Finance Bank for a three-year term, effective Friday. Tripathi, currently chief credit officer, will assume new responsibilities as the bank focuses on building next-generation leadership and institutionalising a long-term succession plan.

articles.headline: Aditya Birla AMC reports 18% decline in Q4 profit to Rs 187.1 crore
articles.summary: Aditya Birla Sun Life Asset Management posted an 18% year-on-year decline in profit after tax to Rs 187.1 crore for the March quarter. Revenue from operations rose 7% YoY to Rs 458.2 crore, and operating profit increased 8% to Rs 252.3 crore.

articles.headline: Shapoorji Pallonji Group to raise Rs 25,400 crore via bonds and loans
articles.summary: Shapoorji Pallonji Group plans to close a Rs 25,400 crore rupee-denominated debt raise by May 15, with 18.75% annual coupons, through USD bonds and bespoke loans. Proceeds will refinance Rs 16,500 crore of Goswami Infratech bonds and partially repay Porteast Investment bondholders, secured against Tata Sons stake.

articles.headline: RBI MPC member Ram Singh indicates no repo rate hike planned for now
articles.summary: Ram Singh, external member of RBI's MPC, states a repo rate hike is not currently being considered, believing the West Asia conflict's inflationary impact will be moderate and gradual. He expects RBI to ensure adequate liquidity in FY27 through measures like OMO purchases.

articles.headline: Gold loan fintechs pivot to own loan books amid tighter RBI norms
articles.summary: Gold loan fintech startups like Indiagold and Oro are shifting from loan sourcing to building their own loan books, raising Rs 50-100 crore in debt, as RBI tightens regulations. This marks a significant change from their previous role as service providers, with co-lending partnerships also being explored.

articles.headline: Egocentric data emerges as key input for training robots, addressing bottleneck
articles.summary: Startups like Humyn AI and FPV Labs are building data pipelines for robotics companies, collecting billions of hours of egocentric data (first-person view) using wearable cameras. This addresses a major bottleneck in robot training, enabling them to learn manipulation and depth perception for safe operation in complex environments.

articles.headline: Reliance Enterprise Intelligence appoints Parminder Singh as CEO
articles.summary: Reliance Enterprise Intelligence Limited (REIL), a joint venture between Reliance Industries and Meta Platforms, appointed Parminder Singh as its chief executive officer. Singh, with two decades of experience from global tech firms, will lead REIL in deploying Meta's Llama models to deliver affordable AI solutions for Indian enterprises and SMBs.

articles.headline: Amazon India invests $300M to expand infrastructure and quick commerce
articles.summary: Amazon India will invest Rs 2,800 crore ($300 million) to strengthen infrastructure and operations, including doubling the footprint of its quick commerce vertical, Amazon Now. The investment will fund new fulfillment and sortation centres, last-mile delivery stations, and technology for faster deliveries across Tier-2 and Tier-3 markets.

articles.headline: Flipkart to launch standalone app for quick commerce vertical, Flipkart Minutes
articles.summary: Flipkart plans to launch a standalone app for its quick commerce vertical, Flipkart Minutes, targeting a pilot in July and full rollout by year-end, ahead of its annual Big Billion Days sale. This move mirrors Swiggy's separate Instamart app, aiming for better visibility and brand recall, according to sources.

articles.headline: Clean power meets new electricity demand, fossil fuel generation drops in 2025
articles.summary: In 2025, new electricity demand was primarily met by clean power sources. Solar generation increased by 636 TWh, wind by 35 TWh, and nuclear by 11 TWh. Fossil fuel generation, including coal, oil, and gas, saw a decrease of 38 TWh, indicating a shift towards renewable energy.

articles.headline: Sony AI develops table tennis robot "Ace" that outperforms expert human players
articles.summary: Scientists at Sony AI have created "Ace," a table tennis robot combining vision sensors, reinforcement learning, and high-speed hardware. The crane-like robot can autonomously locate the ball, determine technique, and return it to an opponent, excelling even against expert human players.

articles.headline: Tata Electronics construction head Reda Masarwa resigns, Gerald Goff appointed
articles.summary: Reda Masarwa, head of construction for Tata Electronics' Rs 91,000 crore fab project, has resigned. Gerald Goff, a US-based advanced technology facilities design consultant, has been appointed to take over the role. This follows resignations of other Israeli executives from the project.

articles.headline: Foreign university campuses in India create over 400 faculty roles
articles.summary: Over a dozen foreign universities setting up campuses in India under NEP 2020 are expected to open 400-450 faculty positions. This creates opportunities for Indian academics overseas to return, especially those facing visa issues or job cuts. Universities aim to blend global faculty with India-based hires, also recruiting administrative staff.

articles.headline: Bharti Group in talks to sell 85% of life insurance business to Prudential
articles.summary: Bharti Group is reportedly negotiating to sell 85% of its life insurance business to Prudential Plc, in a deal potentially valuing the unit at Rs 7-8,000 crore. This move would mark Bharti's exit from the segment and give Prudential a larger foothold in India's insurance market.

articles.headline: Sam Bankman-Fried claims parents didn't draft court motion
articles.summary: One-time crypto king Sam Bankman-Fried, appealing his fraud conviction and 25-year sentence, sought to withdraw a request for a new trial. He claimed his law professor parents only offered "editorial and organisational suggestions" for his court filings, not drafting them, and helped print them as he lacked a word processor.

articles.headline: Woodside Energy CEO Liz Westcott's $10.57M pay package sparks uproar
articles.summary: Australian pension fund HESTA voted against Woodside Energy CEO Liz Westcott’s proposed A$14.8 million ($10.57 million) pay package, calling it "excessive for an incoming CEO." Over 18% of shareholders opposed the remuneration report, while more than one-third rejected a grant of share acquisition rights.

articles.headline: 2026 Met Gala to celebrate "Fashion is Art" with Beyoncé, Bezos as co-chairs
articles.summary: The 2026 Met Gala, themed "Fashion is Art," will raise funds for the Costume Institute. Co-chaired by Beyoncé, Nicole Kidman, Venus Williams, and Anna Wintour, it will feature 400 guests, with individual tickets costing $100,000. Amazon founder Jeff Bezos and Lauren Sánchez are lead sponsors amidst protests.

articles.headline: Research debunks social media claims of mouthwash harming heart health
articles.summary: Social media claims linking mouthwash to high blood pressure by destroying 'good' oral bacteria are largely unsupported by comprehensive research. While some mouthwashes may alter oral microbiome balance or reduce nitrite levels (a nitric oxide precursor), untreated oral infections pose a broader heart disease risk. Experts advise focusing on overall oral hygiene.

articles.headline: Experts advocate for simple, communal hosting to combat loneliness and foster connections
articles.summary: Mental health experts and experienced hosts emphasize that social gatherings combat loneliness, despite host pressure. They suggest simplifying hosting with make-ahead meals, shared tasks, and grazing-style eating to reduce host workload. Vulnerability in opening one's home fosters authentic human connections, which are crucial for happiness and health.

articles.headline: Diego Maradona's childhood home transformed into charity soup kitchen
articles.summary: Football legend Diego Maradona's childhood home in Fiorito, Buenos Aires, has been converted into a soup kitchen, feeding thousands impacted by austerity measures. The initiative honours Maradona's humble beginnings, providing meals and clothing to a poverty-stricken neighbourhood, with Cumbia music playing, reflecting his love for his community.

articles.headline: SantaCon organizer accused of siphoning millions from charity fundraiser
articles.summary: Federal prosecutors accused Stefan Pildes, organizer of New York's annual "SantaCon" bar crawl, of siphoning over half of the $2.7 million supposedly raised for charity between 2019 and 2024. Pildes allegedly spent funds on personal luxuries like concert tickets, a fancy car, and a $100,000 Manhattan apartment, rather than antipoverty groups.

articles.headline: La Rueda de Candombe, Uruguayan music circle, gains international recognition
articles.summary: La Rueda de Candombe, an informal music circle in Montevideo, Uruguay, has evolved from local street corners to international fame, even performing at the Cannes Film Festival. Rooted in 18th-century Afro-Uruguayan traditions, its drum, guitar, and accordion sounds blend with jazz and popular music, symbolizing cultural and political expression.

articles.headline: Japan's cherry tree festivals face rising costs due to inflation
articles.summary: Hanami (flower-viewing picnic) food costs in Japan are at an all-time high, up 25% in six years and 4% since last year. Steamed sweet buns and carbonated drinks saw significant price increases. Broader inflation is prompting reduced spending, while West Asia tensions threaten further cost rises.

articles.headline: Space tourism industry faces crisis as major players halt flights
articles.summary: The space tourism industry is in crisis as Blue Origin pauses flights for two years and Virgin Galactic hasn't flown since June 2024. Despite early predictions of a booming market, limited demand and slow technology scaling, coupled with backlash from celebrity trips, have hampered growth. Chinese companies are now planning to enter the market.

articles.headline: Fossil previously thought to be oldest octopus reclassified as nautilus relative
articles.summary: A 300-million-year-old fossil, Pohlsepia mazonensis, long considered the world's oldest octopus, has been reclassified as a relative of a nautilus. New synchrotron research revealed 11 teeth per row in its radula, unlike the seven or nine found in octopuses, aligning it with a fossil nautiloid from the same Illinois area.

articles.headline: Parametric insurance protects Bad Bunny concerts from weather-related losses
articles.summary: Parametric insurance is emerging as a solution for live events against volatile weather. During Bad Bunny's Colombia concerts, a bespoke policy with on-site weather stations protected against heavy rains, paying out if rainfall exceeded a threshold. This approach, which doesn't require proof of loss, is crucial as extreme weather disrupts global events.

articles.headline: New York Mets losing streak blamed on Mayor Zohran Mamdani's visit
articles.summary: New York Mets fans are blaming Mayor Zohran Mamdani for the team's 12-game losing streak, their longest in over two decades, which began after his visit to Citi Field on April 9. Despite the team's woeful 7-16 record, Mamdani remains optimistic, drawing parallels to the "Curse of the Bambino."

articles.headline: Record Turnout: TN, Bengal See High Voter Participation Amid Electoral Roll Revisions
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu recorded 85.11% turnout, while West Bengal's first phase saw 92.38%, the highest since Independence. These high figures are linked to significant electoral roll revisions, deleting millions of names. Sporadic violence was reported in Bengal, while Tamil Nadu saw a three-cornered contest. Women voters outnumbered men in both states.

articles.headline: Amazon India to Invest ₹2,800 Crore for Associate Well-being, Logistics Expansion
articles.summary: Amazon India announced a ₹2,800 crore investment focused on associate safety, healthcare, and financial well-being. This is part of a broader $35 billion India investment by 2030. Funds will also boost its logistics network with expanded fulfilment centres and delivery stations.

articles.headline: Adani Energy Solutions Reports Q4 Profit Rise Amid Revenue Growth
articles.summary: Adani Energy Solutions posted a 6% net profit increase to ₹684 crore for the March quarter, with revenue up 17% to ₹7,443 crore. For FY26, total income rose 15.9% to ₹28,325 crore. Profitability in transmission and distribution segments drove results, while full-year net profit more than doubled.

articles.headline: Maruti Suzuki Hits Record 23.4 Lakh Units Annual Production
articles.summary: Maruti Suzuki India achieved a record 23.4 lakh passenger vehicle production in FY26, becoming the sole Indian OEM to reach this volume. The company plans to expand capacity to 40 lakh units and is scouting land in Gujarat for a fifth facility.

articles.headline: Classic Legends Eyes 2028 IPO for Global Expansion of Jawa, Yezdi, BSA
articles.summary: Mahindra-backed Classic Legends plans an IPO by 2028 to fund global expansion of brands like BSA in Western markets and Jawa in Eastern Europe. The company sold 57,000 units in FY26 and recently launched new scrambler models.

articles.headline: Fractal's Srikanth Velamakanni Appointed Nasscom Chairperson Amid AI Shift
articles.summary: Nasscom appointed Srikanth Velamakanni, Co-Founder & Group CEO of Fractal, as its new Chairperson. He succeeds Sindhu Gangadharan. Velamakanni leads as the IT sector navigates global economic shifts and AI integration. KPIT's Kishor Patil is Vice-Chairperson.

articles.headline: India Rejects Fuel Price Hike Despite Oil Firm Losses
articles.summary: The government confirmed no plans to raise retail petrol and diesel prices despite state oil companies facing under-recoveries of ₹20/litre on petrol and ₹100/litre on diesel. International crude prices exceeded $113/barrel, but stability is prioritized to manage inflation.

articles.headline: Union Bank of India Q4 Net Profit Rises 7% to ₹5,316 Crore
articles.summary: Union Bank of India reported a 7% rise in Q4 standalone net profit to ₹5,316 crore, aided by lower provisioning and expenses, despite a slight dip in net interest income. Gross NPAs improved to 2.82%. The board proposed a ₹5 dividend per share.

articles.headline: Finance Minister Meets Bank Chiefs Over AI Security Risks
articles.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman discussed AI security threats, particularly from Anthropic's Mythos model, with bank heads. Banks were urged to implement preemptive measures. Mythos has shown capability in identifying digital vulnerabilities, prompting RBI and ministry study of breach risks.

articles.headline: Navi Mumbai Airport International Flights Delayed by West Asia Conflict
articles.summary: International flight operations at Navi Mumbai airport are postponed due to rising jet fuel prices and regional instability from the West Asia conflict. Domestic flights are operating, and terminal construction continues.

articles.headline: India Extends Airspace Ban on Pakistani Aircraft Until May 24
articles.summary: India has extended its airspace closure for Pakistani aircraft until May 24, 2026, a restriction in place for over a year due to security concerns. Pakistan also extended its reciprocal ban on Indian planes, impacting regional flight routes.

articles.headline: India-South Korea Target $50 Billion Trade by 2030
articles.summary: India and South Korea aim to boost bilateral trade to $50 billion by 2030, focusing on deeper economic cooperation and strengthening supply chains during South Korean President Lee Jae Myung's visit.

articles.headline: Paramount Secures Warner Bros. Discovery Approval for $111 Billion Acquisition
articles.summary: Warner Bros. Discovery shareholders approved Paramount's $111 billion (including debt) acquisition at $31 per share. The deal, pending regulatory review, would merge Paramount+ and HBO Max, creating an entertainment giant.

articles.headline: Infosys Beats Revenue Estimates, Warns of AI Impact on Demand
articles.summary: Infosys reported FY26 revenue of $20.16 billion, exceeding expectations, but CEO Salil Parekh cautioned AI automation is compressing demand for traditional services. The company forecasts 1.5-3.5% revenue growth for FY27.

articles.headline: Oil Retailers Face ₹100/litre Loss on Diesel as Prices Remain Frozen
articles.summary: State-run oil firms face ₹20/litre loss on petrol and ₹100/litre on diesel as prices remain static despite crude oil soaring to $113/barrel. The government is prioritizing stable domestic prices to curb inflation, using existing profits to absorb losses.

articles.headline: RBI Warns West Asia Conflict Risks India's Growth and Inflation
articles.summary: The RBI's April bulletin highlighted risks to India's economy from escalating West Asia tensions, including trade disruption and higher energy costs. While FY26 GDP growth is estimated at 7.6%, supply shocks could lead to persistent inflation.

articles.headline: US Trade Representative Calls India 'Tough Nut to Crack' in Negotiations
articles.summary: US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer described India as a "tough nut to crack" during bilateral trade talks in Washington. The negotiations aim to finalize a comprehensive trade agreement, with discussions touching upon agricultural market protection.

articles.headline: RBI Net Buyer of $7.41 Billion Foreign Exchange in February
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India purchased a net $7.41 billion in February to manage rupee volatility, marking the second consecutive month of net dollar buys. The rupee strengthened following the India-US trade deal announcement.

articles.headline: Government Extends Tenures of Bank of India and Bank of Baroda CEOs
articles.summary: The government extended the terms of Rajneesh Karnatak (Bank of India MD) and Debadatta Chand (Bank of Baroda MD) by three years each, ensuring leadership stability at the state-owned banks.

articles.headline: Premji Invest Backs NewEra Hospitals for Maharashtra Expansion
articles.summary: Premji Invest has invested in NewEra Hospitals to expand its multispecialty chain in Maharashtra, planning three new hospitals to increase capacity from 350 to 800 beds for quaternary care.

articles.headline: Rupee Hits Record Low of 94.01 Against US Dollar Amid Market Volatility
articles.summary: The Indian rupee depreciated 23 paise to close at a record low of 94.01 against the US dollar, impacted by rising oil prices and foreign fund outflows due to West Asia tensions.

articles.headline: ReNew to Invest ₹4,200 Crore in Solar Manufacturing in Andhra Pradesh
articles.summary: ReNew Energy Global will invest ₹4,200 crore to establish a 6.5GW solar ingot and wafer plant in Andhra Pradesh, creating over 2,100 jobs and forming part of its ₹82,000 crore state commitment.

articles.headline: France Sees Revival of Traditional Bouillon Restaurants
articles.summary: Traditional 19th-century "bouillon" restaurants are gaining popularity in France, offering affordable, high-quality French meals for under €20, revitalizing culinary traditions and competing with fast-food chains.

articles.headline: PM Modi Inaugurates Key Projects, Addresses Rally in Rajasthan
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated development projects worth over ₹7,000 crore in Rajasthan and addressed a rally, highlighting government achievements including new AI research, industrial hubs, semiconductor manufacturing, and infrastructure.

articles.headline: India Cricket Team Gears Up for Upcoming Series
articles.summary: The Indian cricket team is undergoing intensive training for its upcoming international series, focusing on strategy and team composition.

articles.headline: Government Unveils New Policies for Renewable Energy and MSMEs
articles.summary: The government launched a revised policy to accelerate renewable energy adoption with ambitious targets and a new policy to support MSMEs with easier credit access.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Reserves Verdict on Land Dispute, Rules on Privacy Rights
articles.summary: The Supreme Court concluded hearings on a land dispute, reserving its verdict. In a separate landmark ruling, it clarified citizens' digital privacy rights.

articles.headline: Parliament Discusses and Approves Key Legislative Bills
articles.summary: The ongoing parliamentary session is focused on debating and passing several crucial legislative bills concerning national development, with key approvals granted upon conclusion.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Examines Freedom of Speech Limits
articles.summary: The Supreme Court is examining critical issues related to freedom of speech and expression, including its limitations in the digital age.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Hears Cases on Environmental Protection, Digital Privacy, and Rights
articles.summary: The Supreme Court is hearing cases concerning environmental protection, digital privacy, citizens' fundamental rights, constitutional challenges to legislation, corporate regulation, and judicial independence.

articles.headline: Scientists Develop New Material for Energy Storage and Solar Capture
articles.summary: Researchers have developed a novel material that significantly improves the efficiency and capacity of energy storage devices and enhances solar energy capture.

articles.headline: Climate Change Adaptation Measures and Funding Discussed
articles.summary: Experts discussed strategies for adapting to climate change impacts, focusing on resilient infrastructure and community planning, while a new fund was launched to support developing nations.

articles.headline: Art Exhibitions Highlight Contemporary and Emerging Indian Artists
articles.summary: A major gallery is hosting an exhibition featuring contemporary works by prominent Indian artists, while a festival showcases emerging talent.

articles.headline: PM Modi Reviews Progress of National Schemes and Infrastructure Projects
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi chaired high-level meetings to review the implementation and progress of key national development schemes and infrastructure projects.

articles.headline: Government Boosts Funding for Educational Research and School Infrastructure
articles.summary: The government has increased financial support for research initiatives to improve educational outcomes and allocated funds for the development and renovation of school infrastructure.

articles.headline: Industrial Corridor Project Gains Momentum with Investment Boost
articles.summary: The development of a major industrial corridor is proceeding with significant investment, attracting companies and enhancing regional connectivity.

articles.headline: Manufacturing and Services PMIs Show Strong Expansion
articles.summary: India's Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) for both the manufacturing and services sectors indicates robust expansion in production, new orders, and demand.

articles.headline: Researchers Achieve Breakthroughs in AI Ethics and Fusion Energy
articles.summary: A research team has proposed new ethical frameworks for AI, and scientists report a significant breakthrough in achieving controlled nuclear fusion energy.

articles.headline: Preparations Intensify for Commonwealth and Asian Games
articles.summary: Athletes are intensifying their preparations and participating in qualifying events for the upcoming Commonwealth and Asian Games.

articles.headline: New Bridge Project to Boost Regional Connectivity
articles.summary: Construction has begun on a new bridge designed to significantly improve connectivity and reduce travel times between major regions.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Clarifies Property Rights in Landmark Ruling
articles.summary: The Supreme Court delivered a significant judgment clarifying property rights in a landmark case, emphasizing the right to worship as a protected aspect of conscience.

articles.headline: Educational Institutions Collaborate on Research and Adopt Blended Learning
articles.summary: Multiple educational institutions are signing collaborative agreements for joint research projects and increasingly adopting blended learning models.

articles.headline: Trump Orders US Navy to 'Shoot and Kill' Iranian Boats in Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: President Trump ordered US forces to shoot and kill Iranian boats deploying mines in the Strait of Hormuz following aggressive maneuvers and the seizure of a tanker linked to oil smuggling. Tensions escalate in the critical waterway.

articles.headline: US Navy Secretary John Phelan Resigns Amid Military Leadership Shake-up
articles.summary: Navy Secretary John Phelan is departing his post amid an ongoing leadership overhaul by President Trump. Hung Cao named acting secretary, amid heightened US naval activity in the Persian Gulf.

articles.headline: Karnataka SSLC Results Show Record 94.1% Pass Rate; Seven Score Perfect
articles.summary: Karnataka's SSLC results saw a record 94.1% pass rate, with girls outperforming boys. Seven students achieved a perfect score of 625/625. Digital mark cards are issued via DigiLocker for the first time.

articles.headline: High Court Stays FIR Against Karnataka Home Minister Over Bet Joke
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court stayed an FIR against State Home Minister G. Parameshwara regarding a casual remark about a bet, deeming it lacked 'mens rea' and was not promoting illegal betting.

articles.headline: Thrissur Pooram Scaled Down, Fireworks Cancelled After Fatal Explosion
articles.summary: Thrissur Pooram will be held without fireworks following a fatal explosion at a unit, honoring victims. Rituals will be reduced, with only a single symbolic cracker burst planned.

articles.headline: India Invites German Firms for Defence Co-Development
articles.summary: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh invited German companies to co-develop advanced weapons and sensor technologies, emphasizing partnerships for self-reliance amid global supply chain issues.

articles.headline: Bombay HC Discharges 2006 Malegaon Blast Accused, Citing Case Dead End
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court discharged four accused in the 2006 Malegaon blasts, citing 'diagonally opposite' stories from NIA and ATS and criticizing the special judge's oversight of evidence contradictions.

articles.headline: EU Approves $106 Billion Loan for Ukraine After Hungary Lifts Veto
articles.summary: The EU approved a $106 billion loan package for Ukraine's economic and military needs after Hungary lifted its veto, following negotiations that also saw new sanctions against Russia enacted.

articles.headline: Sanju Samson's Unbeaten Century Powers CSK to Dominant Victory Over MI
articles.summary: Sanju Samson scored 101* off 54 balls, leading Chennai Super Kings to a 103-run victory over Mumbai Indians. CSK posted 207/6, bundling MI out for 104, marking MI's biggest loss by runs in IPL history.

articles.headline: Shanto Century, Mustafizur's Five-Wicket Haul Seal Series for Bangladesh
articles.summary: Najmul Hossain Shanto's 105 and Mustafizur Rahman's 5/43 led Bangladesh to a 55-run win over New Zealand, clinching the ODI series 2-1. New Zealand was bowled out for 210 chasing 266.

articles.headline: Bengal, TN See Record Turnout in First Phase Polls
articles.summary: West Bengal recorded a historic 92.88% turnout in the first phase of Assembly elections, while Tamil Nadu saw 85.15%. Isolated incidents of violence were reported in both states.

articles.headline: HSBC Downgrades India to 'Underweight' Citing Inflation and Oil Risks
articles.summary: HSBC downgraded Indian equities to 'underweight' due to surging energy prices threatening earnings and macro conditions, finding North East Asian peers more attractive.

articles.headline: RBI Deputy Governor Optimistic on Growth, Warns of West Asia Shocks
articles.summary: RBI Deputy Governor Poonam Gupta anticipates growth recovery in H2 FY27, dependent on energy price stability post West Asia conflict. She adopts a 'watch & wait' approach on inflation, seeing low risk of second-round effects.
